author: Besting
text: BOOTSTRAP SOURCE — pin this first (highest-priority detail): the default-config/ contents must be EMBEDDED in the bl binary at compile time (include_dir!/include_str!), written out on first prime when $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/balls/default-config/ is ABSENT; the XDG folder is an OPTIONAL override (org/user customization). Otherwise a fresh `cargo install` or a test binary run from /tmp has no seed and prime fails — reintroducing 'you need a script to get set up,' the exact thing this design removes. So: bootstrap = embedded default; XDG default-config/ = override-if-present.

author: Downfall
text:
HANDOFF from bl-b30a (CLOSED, delivered 2d3121a): the on-disk half of federation is now done — 'make install' lays 'tracker' + 'bl-delivery' beside 'bl' (per-component targets install-core/install-tracker/install-delivery; bare 'make install' = all three), so Edge::resolve finds the siblings on a deployed box.

bl-b30a deliberately did NOT ship a default-config/ folder, deferring to THIS ball's embedded-seed design (see the BOOTSTRAP note above). So the split is: bl-b30a = binaries present + resolvable; bl-4e14 = the seed that makes 'bl prime' register tracker/delivery as landing entries in a fresh config. Federation-on-first-prime is NOT lit until bl-4e14 lands — binaries-beside-bl is necessary but not alone sufficient.

Now unblocked: dep bl-f970 closed (substrate landed, fffffae); this ball is ready to claim.
